codeReview.summary: The backfill handoff now provides an implementation SHA, but it points to an epsilon-tolerance assertion tweak commit rather than the compile-drift parameter-update fix required by this feature/original failure. The traceability gap remains unresolved.

issues.description: Handoff claims compile-drift implementation is commit 61153f...; that commit only adds accuracy: 1e-9 to three XCTAssertEqual cost assertions, not compile-drift parameter updates.

Expected behavior requires implementing SHA(s) touching MultiSourceReconciliationTests compile-drift parameters, but cited implementation commit 61153f... does not touch ProviderUsageRecord parameter wiring (cacheReadTokens/cacheCreationTokens/requestCount), so required traceability is still not auditable to the requested fix scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature metadata routes this misc-infra traceability backfill through switcher-integration-worker, whose procedure is switcher cross-surface implementation/testing oriented and not aligned to traceability-only work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:1777 uses skillName switcher-integration-worker; .factory/skills/switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md:12,21-33 defines cross-surface switcher integration/testing procedure.

summary: Reviewed fix-feature transcript skeleton and both feature diffs (1d56271..., 192a964...) plus the cited implementation SHA (61153f...). The backfill still fails because its claimed implementing commit does not match the compile-drift-parameter scope required by the feature/original failure, so traceability remains incomplete.
